claims.text: Seven of eight Pompe disease patients in this study (all except Subject A1) carried at least one allele with the c.1935C>A (p.D645E) variant in the GAA gene.

citations.quote: Apart from Subject A1, all carried at least one allele with the c.1935C>A (p.D645E) variant in the GAA gene.

claims.text: In all carriers, c.1935C>A is found in cis with c.1726G>A, listed as c.[1935C>A;1726G>A] on the same allele; the variant combinations per subject are: A2 (c.[1935C>A;1726G>A] / c.1781G>A, LOPD), A3 (c.[1935C>A;1726G>A] / c.[842G>T;1726G>A], LOPD), B1 (c.[1935C>A;1726G>A] / c.[1411_1414del;752C>T;761C>T], IOPD), B2 (c.[1935C>A;1726G>A] / c.2024_2026del, IOPD), B3 (c.[1935C>A;1726G>A] / c.1340_1350del, IOPD), B4 (c.[1935C>A;1726G>A] / c.[1935C>A;1726G>A], IOPD), B5 (c.[1935C>A;1726G>A] / c.[1001G>A;1726G>A], IOPD).
All eight patients in the study, including the seven carrying c.1935C>A, were confirmed CRIM-positive.

The three LOPD patients (A2, A3, A3) carrying c.[1935C>A;1726G>A] presented with muscle weakness noted during early adolescence, early infancy, and four years of age respectively, with none exhibiting cardiac manifestations.

citations.quote: Three patients were diagnosed with LOPD (Subject A1-A3) with muscle weakness noted during early adolescence, early infancy, and four years of age, respectively. None exhibited any cardiac manifestations.

claims.text: The five IOPD patients (B1–B5), the majority of whom carry c.[1935C>A;1726G>A], were diagnosed between 8 days and three months of age, primarily presenting with significant cardiac complications.

citations.quote: The remaining five were diagnosed with classic IOPD between 8 days and three months of age, primarily presenting with significant cardiac complications.

claims.text: Subject B4 (IOPD, NBS) is homozygous for the c.[1935C>A;1726G>A] allele combination and developed high ADA titers at 1.6 years after 1.5 years of ERT at 40 mg/kg Q2W; he was independently ambulatory on room air at baseline.
